title: Xxtra Crispy Vietnamese Bacon Bun Thit Nuong
description: A jammy, cheese-pull, comforting Vietnamese dish featuring bacon lardon and confit-tomato, all wrapped up in a crispy bun. Perfect for a quick and satisfying lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: Pork shoulder
ingredients.notes: You can also use pork belly or pork belly cutlets.

ingredients.name: Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: You can also use 1 can of diced tomatoes.

ingredients.name: Coconut sugar
ingredients.notes: You can also use brown sugar.

ingredients.name: Melting cheese
ingredients.notes: You can use mozzarella, cheddar, or a combination of both.

ingredients.name: Bun thit nuong buns
ingredients.notes: You can use traditional or modern buns.

instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Pound the pork shoulder to an even thickness.
Season the pork with salt, pepper, and a sprinkle of fish sauce.
Grill the pork for 5-6 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Meanwhile, grill the bacon lardon slices for 2-3 minutes per side, or until crispy.
Brush the buns with egg wash and toast them on the grill for 2-3 minutes, or until lightly browned.
Slice the grilled pork and bacon into thin strips.
Heat the confit-tomato sauce in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced pork, bacon, and onions to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Transfer the filling to a bun and top with shredded cheese, sliced onions, and a drizzle of confit-tomato sauce.
Place the buns under the broiler for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
